---
title: "Lonely Together Wrangling 1"
author: "Peter Yibo Pan"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(knitr)
theme_set(theme_minimal())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Step 1: Import data & initial analysis
```{r data}
LTdata1 <- read_csv("Lonely Together data 1.csv")
glimpse(LTdata1)

## need to select only relevant columns
LTdata2 <- LTdata1 %>% select(contains("q00")) %>%
  glimpse()
test <- is.na(LTdata2$q0001)
Ineligible 
LTdata3 <- LTdata2 %>% select( -"q0001", -"q0002", -"q0003", -"q0004")
## need to get list of column names & import into vector
q_colnames <- c("age", "gender", "gender_other") ## needs to be full length
varnames <- c(colnames(LTdata3)[1:3])
renaming <- data_frame("variables" = varnames, 
                       "names" = q_colnames )
LTdata4 <- LTdata3 %>% rename_at(
  vars(renaming$variables), ~ renaming$names)
## use vector to rename columns
```


```{r cleaning, echo=FALSE}

## separate into multiple datasets
## questions 1-4 is eligibility screening & consent

## questions 5-12 is demographics (29 total columns)
demographics <- LTdata4 %>% select(1:29)

## questions 7 gender identity and 12 racial identity needs to be condensed into single value

## questions 13, 14, and 15 are RHI (37 total columns)
RHI <- LTdata4 %>% select(30:66)
## questions 16 is DJGLS (11 total columns)
DJG <- LTdata4 %>% select(67:77)


## detect for incomplete surveys & missing values & extreme values (age)
```


```{r scores}




```
